records.ety: [OF. copeiz, fr. coper, couper, to cut, F. couper, fr. cop, coup, colp, a blow, F. coup, L. colaphus, fr. Gr. ko`lafos. Cf. Copse, and cf. Coup, Coupee.]

defs: A grove of small growth; a thicket of brushwood; a wood cut at certain times for fuel or other purposes. See Copse.
quotes: The rate of coppice lands will fall, upon the discovery of coal mines.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Coppiced (kp"pst); p. pr. & vb. n. Coppicing (kp"p*sng).]

defs: To cause to grow in the form of a coppice; to cut back (as young timber) so as to produce shoots from stools or roots.
